title: eriogonum umbellatum subsp. covillei
intro: Eriogonum umbellatum subsp. covillei, also known as Coville's sulfur buckwheat, is a subspecies of the Eriogonum umbellatum plant, native to North America. This shrubby perennial has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits of Coville's sulfur buckwheat include its potential to treat digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its roots and leaves can be used fresh or dried for medicinal purposes.
